meta.tagline: The gifting platform that turns a family's love into something worth keeping.

why.headline: The Problem Is Universal. The Solution Has Never Existed.
why.subheadline: Every year, billions are spent on gifts nobody wanted, by people who felt obliged to guess.

problem.label: The Broken Status Quo

stats.caption: spent on unwanted gifts in the UK every year
of UK adults feel stressed buying gifts
more household waste in December alone

problem.copy: The gifting market is broken in a way that feels personal to everyone. The giver feels pressure, anxiety, and the fear of getting it wrong. The receiver gets a pile of things they didn't choose. Everyone involved has quietly wished there was a better way — but no product has made it simple enough to actually change behaviour.

solution.label: Why Kindled Wins
solution.headline: One coordinated pot. Real things people actually want.
solution.copy: Kindled replaces the chaos of uncoordinated gift-buying with a single, beautiful 'Fire' — a shared pot where everyone chips in. The receiver chooses what they want. Contributors give confidently. The moment of reveal becomes a shared occasion rather than an awkward exchange.

points.title: The 1-in-6 Viral Rule
points.body: Every time one person creates a Fire, they invite an average of 6 contributors. Each of those contributors experiences the platform — and the majority go on to create their own Fire. This is not paid acquisition. It is organic, peer-led growth built into the core product loop.

points.title: Removes the Awkwardness
points.body: Asking for money as a gift is socially uncomfortable. Kindled reframes it entirely — you are not asking for cash, you are curating a meaningful experience that your loved ones can contribute to with pride. The platform provides the social cover that makes participation feel natural.

points.title: The Right Moment in Culture
points.body: Post-pandemic consumers have fundamentally shifted. Personalisation is now an expectation, not a premium. Generic vouchers feel lazy. Experiences beat objects. Kindled is the product this cultural shift has been waiting for.

steps.title: Host Creates a Fire
steps.body: In under two minutes, the receiver (or someone acting on their behalf) builds a wishlist — a single goal, or a collection of dreams. They set a target, choose a reveal date, and share a link. No app download required for contributors.

steps.title: Givers Contribute
steps.body: Family, friends, and colleagues chip in whatever feels right — £10, £50, £200. Open Banking makes the payment frictionless and fee-light. Each contribution is confirmed in seconds. Contributors can optionally leave a message or a short video that becomes part of the reveal.

steps.title: The Reveal Moment
steps.body: On the chosen date — a birthday morning, Christmas Day, an anniversary — the receiver opens their Fire. A personalised AI-generated cinematic video plays. The messages and faces of everyone who contributed appear. It is not a notification. It is an occasion.

points.title: Open Banking Is Mature
points.body: UK Open Banking adoption crossed 10 million users in 2023. Account-to-account payments are now fast, trusted, and dramatically cheaper than card rails. Kindled is built on this infrastructure from day one — giving us a structural cost advantage over any competitor still routing through Visa and Mastercard.

points.title: AI Has Unlocked a New Category of Emotion
points.body: The AI-generated reveal video is not a gimmick — it is the product's emotional centrepiece. Two years ago this was impossible at consumer scale. Today it costs pennies per video and creates a moment that people genuinely share. That shareability is the distribution engine.

points.title: The Market Is Fragmented and Waiting
points.body: There is no dominant, consumer-facing group-gifting product in the UK. Existing solutions are either clunky (PayPal pools), B2B only (corporate gifting platforms), or solve a narrow slice (GoFundMe is charity-focused). Kindled is the first product designed for the everyday gifting moment — built beautifully and end-to-end.

engine.headline: Two Phases. One Compounding Machine.
engine.subheadline: Phase 1 builds the flywheel. Phase 2 monetises the data it generates.

phase1.label: Phase 1 — Foundation
phase1.tagline: Build the viral loop. Capture the intent signal.
phase1.summary: The primary objective of Phase 1 is user acquisition through product virality, not paid marketing. Every Fire created is a distribution event. Every contributor is a future host. The platform compounds without ad spend.

pillars.title: The Viral Loop
pillars.body: Host creates Fire → invites 6 contributors → contributors experience the product → 1 in 6 creates their own Fire. At scale, this produces organic growth curves that paid acquisition cannot replicate economically.

pillars.title: Intent Data Capture
pillars.body: When a user adds a wishlist item, they are declaring exactly what they want to buy — category, price point, brand affinity — before the transaction occurs. This is the most valuable signal in commerce. Kindled captures it at the moment of highest intent and at zero marginal cost.

pillars.title: Open Banking Revenue
pillars.body: A 0.5% platform fee on contributions processed via Open Banking. Structurally lower than card processing fees. Passed through to contributors as a better experience, retained by the platform as margin.

pillars.title: Cashback Flywheel
pillars.body: Sponsored retailers pay to have their products featured on the platform's wishlist catalogue. Contributors who purchase via Kindled earn cashback, which they can use on future contributions. This creates a closed-loop economy that increases contribution frequency and average order value.

phase2.label: Phase 2 — Scale
phase2.tagline: Sell the signal. Power the prediction engine.
phase2.summary: Phase 2 activates the B2B revenue layer that Phase 1 quietly built. The intent data accumulated during Phase 1 is now a licensed asset — sold to retailers, brands, and programmatic advertisers who pay significant premiums for verified purchase intent at category level.

pillars.title: Predictive B2B Licensing
pillars.body: Aggregated, anonymised intent data is packaged as a real-time signal feed. A furniture retailer can buy a segment of users who declared sofa intent in the last 30 days, in the £400–£900 range, before those users have spent anywhere. This is a category that currently doesn't exist at consumer scale.

pillars.title: Temporal Advantage Windows
pillars.body: Between the date a pot is funded and the reveal date (typically 10–20 days), Kindled runs a reverse-auction among relevant retailers. The winning brand gets placement at the reveal moment and a verified buyer signal. CPM rates in this window are 3–5x standard display advertising.

pillars.title: Stripe Connect Payouts
pillars.body: As contribution volume scales, the platform transitions to full Stripe Connect (Express) — enabling instant host payouts, simplified compliance, and the infrastructure for a future financial services layer (BNPL, savings products, gift registries).

pillars.title: Enterprise Gifting API
pillars.body: Corporate HR and rewards teams pay to white-label Kindled Fires for employee milestones — work anniversaries, retirement, new baby. B2B contracts at £1,000–£10,000 per month with zero CAC (pure outbound sales).

flywheel.headline: Why Kindled Compounds
flywheel.subheadline: Every reveal is a free advert. Every giver is a future host. The loop turns itself — no ad budget required.

loop.title: A host lights a Fire
loop.body: Someone sets up a pot for a birthday, baby shower or Christmas — in 90 seconds, for free.

loop.title: They rally ~6 people
loop.body: One share in the family chat pulls an average of six contributors onto the platform.

loop.title: Givers chip in
loop.body: Each pays via Open Banking in seconds — and quietly declares exactly what they'd love too.

loop.title: The reveal goes off
loop.body: A cinematic, shareable moment. People screenshot it. It reaches new faces at zero ad spend.

loop.title: Givers become hosts
loop.body: Around 1 in 6 starts their own Fire — and the loop begins again, bigger each turn.

multiplier.label: people per Fire — every one a future host

compounding.label: What one Fire becomes
compounding.caption: Three turns of the loop from a single Fire. No paid acquisition — the product is the marketing.

power.title: Zero-CAC growth
power.body: The reveal moment is the advert. Distribution is built into the product, not bought.

power.title: Intent at the source
power.body: Every wishlist item is a purchase declared before any money is spent — the most valuable signal in commerce.

power.title: Silent monetisation
power.body: 0.5% on every contribution today; licensed intent data and reveal-window ads tomorrow. No ads, no subscriptions.

journey.headline: Four Moments. One Memory.
journey.subheadline: This is what Kindled feels like from the inside.

stages.narrative: Sarah is turning 40. She knows exactly what she wants — a weekend spa break she'd never buy herself. She opens Kindled, names her Fire, sets the target, adds the experience. It takes ninety seconds. She shares the link in the family WhatsApp. She feels none of the awkwardness of asking. She feels the quiet joy of being understood.
stages.insight: The insight: people don't want to be modest about what they want. They just need social permission to be specific. Kindled provides that permission.
steps: Sets target amount and reveal date
Shares a single link — no app required for contributors
Receives real-time notifications as the pot fills

stages.narrative: Sarah's brother James gets the link. He clicks it. He can see exactly what the spa break costs, how much has already been raised, and what a meaningful contribution looks like. He chips in £50 via Open Banking — no card details, confirmed in four seconds. He leaves a short voice message. He feels good about this gift in a way he rarely has before.
stages.insight: The insight: givers want to feel their contribution matters. A coordinated pot with a visible target makes every amount feel significant, not arbitrary.
steps: Opens link on any device — no account required
Sees the goal, the progress, and suggested contribution amounts
Pays via Open Banking in four seconds
Optionally leaves a message or short video

stages.narrative: On Sarah's birthday morning, she opens her Fire. A full-screen, AI-generated video plays — her name, the spa, the faces and voices of everyone who contributed. Her sister's video message plays automatically. It is cinematic, personal, and completely unexpected. She cries. She shares it. Eight people who see it immediately think: I want this for someone I love.
stages.insight: The insight: the reveal is the distribution engine. A shareable emotional moment is worth more than any ad campaign. This is how Kindled grows.
steps: Receives notification: 'Your Fire is fully kindled'
Watches messages and tributes from every contributor
Shares the moment — generating organic top-of-funnel awareness

stages.narrative: Three of the twelve people who watched Sarah's reveal video go on to create their own Fire within the following month. James — who gifted £50 — realises this is how he wants to do gifts from now on. He creates a Fire for his son's birthday. The loop closes. The platform grows without an acquisition budget.
stages.insight: The insight: the product's best feature is the feeling it creates. Feelings become habits. Habits become retention. This is a gifting platform with social network dynamics.
steps: Repeat contribution behaviour builds cashback balance
Social proof drives referral without explicit referral mechanics
Each Fire is a new distribution event for the platform

roadmap.headline: Execution in Phases. Compounding by Design.
roadmap.subheadline: Each phase unlocks the next. Nothing is speculative — every milestone has a clear strategic purpose.

milestones.title: Core Product Launch
milestones.why: The MVP establishes the product loop: Host creates Fire → Givers contribute → Reveal moment. Without this working end-to-end, nothing else matters. Speed to market is the priority — the viral loop cannot start until the first Fire is created.

milestones.title: Viral Loop Instrumentation
milestones.why: We need to know whether the 1-in-6 conversion is happening in practice. This milestone instruments the full contributor-to-host funnel, enabling us to optimise the moments where contributors are most likely to create their own Fire.

milestones.title: Intent Data Architecture
milestones.why: The intent data layer is the foundation of Phase 2 revenue. Every wishlist item added is a signal. We build the capture, categorisation, and anonymisation infrastructure now — so that by the time we go to B2B market in Phase 2, we have 12 months of clean, exportable data.

milestones.title: Open Banking & Fee Engine
milestones.why: Open Banking is our structural cost advantage. A2A payments cost ~80% less than card processing. This milestone locks in the payment infrastructure and the 0.5% platform fee — the first revenue stream — before scaling contribution volume.
deliverables: Fee calculation engine (tested to financial standard)

milestones.title: AI Reveal — General Availability
milestones.why: The AI reveal video is the product's emotional centrepiece and primary distribution mechanism. Once it is live and shareable, every funded Fire becomes a piece of branded content that reaches new audiences organically. This milestone is the ignition point for top-of-funnel growth.

milestones.title: Cashback Catalogue & Retailer Integration
milestones.why: The cashback catalogue creates the closed-loop economy: contributors earn cashback, which they spend on future contributions, which generates more data, which attracts more retailers. This is the flywheel that makes Phase 2 possible.

milestones.title: B2B Intent Data Licensing
milestones.why: By this point we have 12+ months of anonymised intent data at category level. We package it as a licensed signal feed for retailers, brands, and programmatic buyers. This is a high-margin, recurring revenue stream with no marginal cost per sale.

milestones.title: Temporal Advantage Windows
milestones.why: The 10–20 day window between pot funding and reveal is a uniquely valuable advertising moment. The audience has verified purchase intent for a specific item in a specific price range. We run a reverse-auction in this window. CPM rates are 3–5x standard display.

milestones.title: Enterprise Gifting API
milestones.why: Corporate HR teams spend significant budget on employee gifting — work anniversaries, retirements, new babies. Kindled is a natural fit. White-labelled Fires with company branding, managed by HR, at enterprise contract values of £1,000–£10,000/month.

milestones.title: Financial Services Expansion
milestones.why: A platform that handles family finances around milestone moments is naturally positioned for adjacent financial products: gift savings plans, buy-now-pay-later contributions, and eventually a Kindled debit card that earns cashback on all spending. This is the long-term moat.
deliverables: Partnership with a licensed e-money institution
